scene: 1 — Maya turns her finished study into a call for a firm
detail: Status redundancy (READY TO SOLICIT badge + 'reviewed' label + a third restated sentence 'Finished study — ready to recruit a survey firm against this coverage') plus a lower frame that is ~50% empty leave the opener functional-not-polished — nothing embarrassing, nothing delightful.
fix_recommendation: Collapse the three restatements of 'ready to recruit a survey firm' into one, and tighten the empty lower frame (surface the candidate-plans list or a next-step hint).

scene: 3 — Amina reads the open call and ticks the area she can staff
detail: ADVISORY. The concept_claim asserts submission is blocked until an area is chosen with the message 'Select at least one coverage area to submit,' but that error/blocked state is NOT visible in this frame — nothing is ticked, Submit appears active, no error string shown. The static helper line 'Select one or more — required to submit' is present and consistent, so it is not self-contradicting, but the asserted blocked behavior is unverifiable from this still.
fix_recommendation: Add a scene/frame showing the blocked submit with the exact 'Select at least one coverage area to submit.' message so the gating half of S5 is demonstrated, not just asserted.

scene: 4 — Maya sees the firm tied to the coverage she designed
detail: Incoherent state combo: STATUS shows a green 'Awarded' pill while RECOMMENDATION reads 'Not reviewed' AND a live 'Review' action is still offered — an already-awarded item cannot simultaneously be un-reviewed and still invite review (you review before you award). The ACTIONS strip also mixes verbs with a state label ('View / Review / Awarded'), so 'Awarded' masquerades as a sibling action.
fix_recommendation: Make status the single source of truth: when a response is Awarded, suppress/disable the 'Review' action, resolve RECOMMENDATION to the actual decision (or hide it), and move 'Awarded' out of the action strip into the STATUS cell only so ACTIONS holds exclusively verbs.

scene: 4 — Maya sees the firm tied to the coverage she designed
detail: The single-row 7-column table leaves SCORE ('-') and RECOMMENDATION columns visibly empty (~30% blank), the EOI/Active header pills float far right of the title across dead whitespace, and the captured mouse cursor overlaps and occludes the breadcrumb text.
fix_recommendation: Add explicit em-dash empty-states for the SCORE/RECOMMENDATION columns, pull the EOI/Active pills closer to the title, and re-capture without the cursor occluding the breadcrumb.

scene: 4 — Maya sees the firm tied to the coverage she designed
detail: ADVISORY. The narrative frames this as a FRESH application Maya is reviewing/comparing ('the loop from a study plan to a real applicant closed'), but the row is already 'Awarded' with RECOMMENDATION 'Not reviewed' — the captured state is ahead of the narrated review moment and internally contradicts itself (awarded yet not reviewed). The screenshot depicts a post-decision state, not the live review-and-compare moment the scene narrates.
fix_recommendation: Re-capture this scene with the response in a pre-award state (status 'Submitted' or 'Under review', Review action live, no Awarded pill) to match the narrated reviewing/comparing moment.

scene: 4 — Maya sees the firm tied to the coverage she designed
detail: Static frame is clean and loaded (no spinners/error), but the contradictory live 'Review' action on an already-awarded row implies a click path that leads to a confusing state — latent interaction friction.
fix_recommendation: Ensure awarded rows present a single coherent next action (e.g. 'View award') so a click never lands the owner in a review flow for an already-decided response.

scene: 5 — Maya looks ahead to standing up the real opportunity
detail: The closing beat is the planning-vs-execution boundary, but nothing in the frame signals 'next step' or 'not yet built' — the 5-second read is 'firm awarded,' a wrong read for this beat. The concept lives only in narration.
fix_recommendation: Add a visible forward affordance — a disabled 'Provision Connect opportunity (coming soon)' control or a post-award callout — so the UI itself carries the not-yet-built next step instead of leaving it to narration.

scene: 5 — Maya looks ahead to standing up the real opportunity
detail: Same incoherent state combo as scene 4: an 'Awarded' row still offers a 'Review' action and shows RECOMMENDATION 'Not reviewed'; STATUS 'Awarded' is duplicated by the green Awarded pill in ACTIONS; SCORE is empty.
fix_recommendation: On award, suppress/disable the Review action, resolve 'Not reviewed' to the actual review state, and collapse the duplicate Awarded status/action so the row is internally consistent.

scene: 5 — Maya looks ahead to standing up the real opportunity
detail: Traces to spine S7 (recruiting/reviewing a firm is the shipped step; the row is a real awarded response captured for review), but the frame omits S7's whole second half — the not-yet-built provisioning tail — so the grounding is only half-present on screen.
fix_recommendation: Surface the 'next step, not yet built' half of S7 in the frame (e.g. the disabled provisioning affordance from the concept_clarity fix) rather than leaving it to narration.

scene: 5 — Maya looks ahead to standing up the real opportunity
detail: ADVISORY. The 'next step, not yet built' boundary is asserted only in narration; the frame shows a live, already-taken 'Awarded' action, which reads as completion and undercuts 'field execution is the aspirational next step.'
fix_recommendation: Make the aspirational boundary visible (disabled provisioning control / 'no live opportunity yet' note) so the claim is coherent with the frame.

scene: 5 — Maya looks ahead to standing up the real opportunity
detail: scene_5.png is byte-identical to scene_4.png (same md5) — a second consecutive identical still with zero visual progression; the closing beat reuses the prior frame, so the viewer gets no signal a new beat occurred.
fix_recommendation: Give scene 5 a distinct frame (scroll, highlight/hover the Award action, or render a post-award state with the deferred-provisioning affordance) so the closing beat advances visually.
